segments.text: They brought the ark of God and set it inside the tent that David had pitched for it, and they presented burnt offerings and fellowship offerings before God.
After David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and fellowship offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord.
Then he gave a loaf of bread, a cake of dates and a cake of raisins to each Israelite man and woman.
He appointed some of the Levites to minister before the ark of the Lord, to extol, thank, and praise the Lord, the God of Israel:

Asaph was the chief, and next to him in rank were Zechariah, then Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-Edom and Jeiel. They were to play the lyres and harps, Asaph was to sound the cymbals,
and Benaiah and Jahaziel the priests were to blow the trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
That day David first appointed Asaph and his associates to give praise to the Lord in this manner:
Give praise to the Lord, proclaim his name; make known among the nations what he has done.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Sing to him, sing praise to him; tell of all his wonderful acts.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Look to the Lord and his strength; seek his face always.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Remember the wonders he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he pronounced,
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: you his servants, the descendants of Israel, his chosen ones, the children of Jacob.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: He remembers his covenant forever, the promise he made, for a thousand generations,
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: the covenant he made with Abraham, the oath he swore to Isaac.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as an everlasting covenant:
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: To you I will give the land of Canaan as the portion you will inherit.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: When they were but few in number, few indeed, and strangers in it,
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: they wandered from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: He allowed no one to oppress them; for their sake he rebuked kings:
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Do not touch my anointed ones; do my prophets no harm.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Sing to the Lord, all the earth; proclaim his salvation day after day.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous deeds among all peoples.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For great is the Lord and most worthy of praise; he is to be feared above all gods.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For all the gods of the nations are idols, but the Lord made the heavens.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Splendor and majesty are before him; strength and joy are in his dwelling place.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Ascribe to the Lord, all you families of nations, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name; bring an offering and come before him. Worship the Lord in the splendor of his holiness.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Tremble before him, all the earth! The world is firmly established; it cannot be moved.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Let the heavens rejoice, let the earth be glad; let them say among the nations, “The Lord reigns!”
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Let the sea resound, and all that is in it; let the fields be jubilant, and everything in them!
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Let the trees of the forest sing, let them sing for joy before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Cry out, “Save us, God our Savior; gather us and deliver us from the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name, and glory in your praise.”
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.
Then all the people said
Amen
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: Praise the Lord.
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: David left Asaph and his associates before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to minister there regularly, according to each day’s requirements.
He also left Obed-Edom and his sixty-eight associates to minister with them. Obed-Edom son of Jeduthun, and also Hosah, were gatekeepers.
David left Zadok the priest and his fellow priests before the tabernacle of the Lord at the high place in Gibeon
to present burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar of burnt offering regularly, morning and evening, in accordance with everything written in the Law of the Lord, which he had given Israel.
With them were Heman and Jeduthun and the rest of those chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the Lord,
for his love endures forever.
segments.speaker: Heman and Jeduthun

segments.text: Heman and Jeduthun were responsible for the sounding of the trumpets and cymbals and for the playing of the other instruments for sacred song. The sons of Jeduthun were stationed at the gate.
Then all the people left, each for their own home, and David returned home to bless his family.
